headline: Teacher's WhatsApp Campaign Reaches 8,500 Girls With HPV Vaccines
meta_description: After losing his sister to cervical cancer, a Gambian head teacher mobilized parents through school WhatsApp to achieve 98% consent for HPV vaccination.
summary_short: A Gambian head teacher turned grief into action, using his school's WhatsApp group to secure parent consent for a catch-up HPV vaccination campaign that reached 8,500 gi…
body:
A Gambian head teacher transformed personal tragedy into public health success, using his school's WhatsApp group to help achieve 98% parent consent for a catch-up HPV vaccination campaign that ultimately vaccinated 8,500 girls.

Motivated by the loss of his sister to cervical cancer, the educator recognized an opportunity to protect the young women in his community. Rather than rely on traditional outreach methods, he leveraged the communication tool already connecting parents—the school's WhatsApp group—to build trust and secure consent at an unusually high rate.

The campaign's success demonstrates how existing community networks can be repurposed to advance preventive health. By meeting parents where they already gather, the head teacher turned a school communication platform into a lifeline, helping ensure that thousands of girls now have protection against cervical cancer.
quick_facts: Gambian head teacher lost his sister to cervical cancer
Used school WhatsApp group to reach parents
